description: Discovered systematic relationship between NTL indicator type and optimal socioeconomic proxy: NTL均值(mean) strongly correlates with population density (ρ=0.9630) but weakly with GDP总量 (ρ=0.2758); TNTL(sum) strongly correlates with GDP总量 (ρ=0.8188). This validates the fundamental distinction between intensity metrics (mean, per-unit-area) and aggregate metrics (sum, total) in NTL-based socioeconomic estimation.
lesson_learned: NTL indicator selection rule: (a) Use NTL均值 (mean) when estimating density-type variables (population density, urbanization intensity, electrification rate); (b) Use TNTL (sum) when estimating aggregate-type variables (GDP总量, total population, total energy consumption). Using the wrong indicator type leads to poor correlation (e.g., NTL均值 vs GDP: ρ=0.28) even when the correct indicator works well (TNTL vs GDP: ρ=0.82).
corrective_action_taken: Established 3-dimensional comparison framework: (1) NTL均值 vs population density ρ=0.9630; (2) NTL均值 vs GDP ρ=0.2758; (3) TNTL vs GDP ρ=0.8188. All validated on China 34 provinces, 2020.
prevention_mechanism: When designing NTL-based socioeconomic estimation workflows, always match indicator type to target variable type: mean→density, sum→aggregate. This should be documented as a mandatory decision gate in indicator estimation workflows.
impact: Provides methodological foundation for GDP estimation (use TNTL, not ANTL), population density estimation (use NTL均值), and general NTL indicator selection for any socioeconomic variable.
